calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lauren Grause for the Beneficiary of the Estate of Nolan Edwards. Elton Blum with associated Counsel for Law Finance Group. Dominick Libonati for Renee Jackson (Beneficiary). Bernardo Silva for Leroy Johnson (Petitioner/Admiistrator).

Mr. Labonati reported that the parties settled with regard to the fees for Law Finance Group and that they agreed to a reduction in the fees. Mr. Silva explained the extent of services he provided to support his fee request. The Court took Mr. Silva's fees under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR#12891. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-27-2009 Continued To Aug-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Continued To Jul-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bernardo Silva for Leroy Johnson (Petitioner/Administrator). Dominick Libonati for Renee Jackson (Beneficiary). John Rosenbaum for LawFinance Group (Licensed Lender/Creditor of the Heirs).

The Court stated that it wanted to focus on the interest amount. Mr. Libonati stated that when he filed the objection he did not realize that it was an exempt corporation, that the interest should be whatever is allowed under the law or in the agreement, that the claim is usurious, and the maximum should be 7 percent. Mr. Rosenbaum presented his argument stating in part that he was not served with the objections and that he wished to be served, that the contract with the heirs should be subject to arbitration, that the code provides exemption of usuary law firms, and that in light of the exemption he called Mr. Libonati and asked him to withdraw his objection. Mr. Libonati stated that he had sent the objections to Mr. Rosenbaum.
Discussions were held regarding the amounts of interest, what the proper rate of interest is, and what the amount due by a "date certain" would be. The Court inquired as to what the per diem would be. Mr. Rosebaum stated that $410,443.16 is due to LawFinance per the 1.9 percent interest rate which comes out to be 26 percent per year. Mr. Libonati stated that he continued to object and had tried to talk compromise. The Court stated that it wanted Points and Authorities from Mr. Rosenbaum arguing the law. Mr. Rosenbaum's Points and Authorities are to be filed and served by June 26, 2009. Mr. Libonati's reply, if any, is to be filed and served by the close of business on July 2, 2009. The matter was continued to July 27, 2009. Discussions were held regarding LawFinance continuing to charge interest. The Court stated that would be left up to counsel to discuss.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (742-744 Hayes Street, S.F.)
Off Calendar Apr-05-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bernardo Silva (Counsel for LeRoy Johnson, Personal Representative).

After announcement of the sale and sale terms by Mr. Silva and after extensive overbidding, the Court confirmed the sale of the property at the final overbid of $1,205,000.00. Additional bond was set at $145,000. Mr. Silva is to prepare the Order.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Loos; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.
